results.message: Data texture uses a color-managed color space.
results.title: Data textures must use Raw color space
results.why: Scalar maps such as roughness, masks, normal, bump, displacement, and opacity must not be transformed as color data because color transforms alter numeric values.

results.message: Color texture is configured as non-color data.
results.title: Color textures must use a color-managed color space
results.why: Base color, specular color, and emission maps represent color information. They should remain color-managed so viewport, lookdev, and final renders interpret them consistently.

results.message: Displacement texture file is missing or cannot be resolved.
results.title: Displacement texture file must exist
results.why: Missing displacement maps can change object silhouettes, create render holes, or make farm renders diverge from approved lookdev. Displacement dependencies must be resolved before publish or farm submission.

results.message: Displacement amount is above the configured production risk budget.
results.title: Displacement amount must stay within the risk budget
results.why: Excessive displacement can explode render time, memory, tessellation, and silhouette changes. Keeping amount within a profile-defined budget reduces farm risk.

results.message: Multiple texture nodes reference the same resolved texture path.
results.title: Duplicate texture file usage should be reviewed
results.why: Duplicate texture nodes that point to the same file increase graph complexity and make material reviews harder. Reporting duplicate file usage helps consolidate redundant texture nodes when it is safe to do so.

results.message: Optimized texture derivative is missing.
results.title: Optimized texture file should exist when tracked
results.why: Missing optimized texture derivatives such as .tx files can increase render startup cost, texture IO, and farm memory pressure. When scanner metadata tracks an optimized derivative, the derivative should exist.

results.message: Optimized texture derivative is stale compared with its source texture.
results.title: Optimized texture derivative should be fresh
results.why: Stale optimized derivatives can render older pixels than the source texture and make local lookdev diverge from farm rendering. Freshness should be reported when scanner metadata detects a stale optimized derivative.

results.message: Material shader graph has more nodes than the configured budget.
results.title: Material graph node count must stay within budget
results.why: Large shader graphs are harder to review, slower to evaluate, and more likely to hide expensive or broken branches. Budget checks keep material networks maintainable before publish.

results.message: Material shader graph is deeper than the configured budget.
results.title: Material graph depth must stay within budget
results.why: Deep shader graphs are costly to inspect and can signal overly complex procedural or layered networks. Depth budgets help identify networks that need TD review.

results.message: Material has no assigned geometry.
results.title: Material should be assigned to production geometry
results.why: Unassigned materials are often dead shader networks. Reporting them keeps assets easier to review and prevents stale lookdev nodes from hiding in production scenes.

results.message: Shading engine has no assigned members.
results.title: Shading engine should have assigned members
results.why: Empty shading engines usually indicate dead assignment state or leftover shader network scaffolding. Reporting them helps clean asset shading graphs before publish.

results.message: Production geometry is assigned to the default material.
results.title: Production geometry should not use the default material
results.why: Default material assignments usually mean geometry lost its intended lookdev assignment. Detecting this prevents publishes with unshaded or fallback-looking production geometry.

results.message: Texture dependency is not using the latest detected version.
results.title: Texture version should be latest available
results.why: Keeping texture dependencies on the latest approved version helps keep publishes consistent with current lookdev handoff.

results.message: Texture file is missing or cannot be resolved.
results.title: Texture file must exist
results.why: Missing texture files render incorrectly in Maya and on the render farm. Blocking them before publish prevents wasted farm time and broken lookdev handoffs.

results.message: Texture path points to a local drive and may not resolve on the farm.
results.title: Texture path must not use a local drive root
results.why: Local drive paths are machine-specific. Render farm workers and other artists may not be able to resolve them, causing missing textures or inconsistent renders.

results.message: Texture path points to a user-local folder such as Desktop, Downloads, or Temp.
results.title: Texture path must not use a user-local folder
results.why: User-local folders are not production storage. They are commonly unavailable to farm machines and are unsafe for publish handoff.

results.message: Texture path is outside the approved project texture roots.
results.title: Texture path must use an approved project root
results.why: Project-rooted paths keep scene dependencies portable between artists, publish tools, and render farm workers. Paths outside approved roots are harder to audit and reproduce.

results.message: UDIM texture set has missing tiles.
results.title: UDIM texture set must not have missing tiles
results.why: Missing UDIM tiles create visible render holes or fallback texture samples. Detecting incomplete tile sets before publish prevents broken assets and wasted farm renders.
